commit_scope: Make parsed BML BodySource execution walk supported statements in source order, including receiver calls with one argument followed by constructor assignments.

local_validation.notes: The sequence proof executes m_hResultStack.Push(m_hResult) before m_hResult = ParseStack() from original BMF.StartResultStack. The earlier constructor-only proof was updated to provide the stack receiver now that generic execution runs the full body.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: A .bml method body with mixed supported statement forms now executes in source order instead of choosing a single feature class for the whole body.
test_flows: python3 scripts/audit_bml_bmf_kernel_natives.py before commit to prove no new BML/BMF host-native semantics were introduced.

phase_gate.reason: This adds source-order execution for currently supported statement forms. Full thesis parity still requires nested call arguments, conditionals, loops, casts, comparisons, exceptions, and full BMF rule execution from the known-working companion code.

evidence_refs: form/form-stdlib/grammars/bml.fk defines bml-body-source-exec-sequence-loop and receiver-call1 support.
form/form-stdlib/tests/bml-thesis-bmf-start-result-stack-sequence-proof.fk proves Push runs before ParseStack assignment in original BMF.StartResultStack.
